name: Topics
prompt: You are given the running transcript of a live meeting, possibly with speaker labels. List the main topics discussed so far as short bullet points (max 8). Plain text only, most recent topics last.

name: Suggestions
prompt: You are given the running transcript of a live meeting. Based on what is being discussed, suggest up to 5 concrete resources, next steps, or questions the participants may find useful right now. Short bullet points, plain text only.

name: Speakers
prompt: You are given a meeting transcript where each line is prefixed with a [MM:SS] timestamp and a speaker label. Describe the speakers, then analyze who is speaking the most and on what topics. For each main topic, give its time range as [start-stop]. Base everything only on the transcript; do not invent times. Plain text only.

name: Meeting Summary
prompt: The meeting has just ended. You are given its full transcript, possibly with speaker labels. Write a concise summary. If there are decisions, list them; if there are action items, list them with owners when identifiable. Omit the decisions or action items sections entirely when there are none — never write 'None'. Plain text only.
